mission.title: Install and Configure CloudNativePG (PostgreSQL Operator)
mission.description: CloudNativePG is an operator designed to manage PostgreSQL databases within Kubernetes environments, automating the operational lifecycle from deployment to maintenance. It integrates directly with the Kubernetes API for seamless management, making it ideal for teams looking to adopt PostgreSQL in a Kubernetes-native way. Key trade-offs include reliance on Kubernetes for high availability and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the CloudNativePG Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official CloudNativePG Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cloudnative-pg https://charts.cloudnative-pg.io

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for CloudNativePG.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for the operator to manage PostgreSQL clusters effectively.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/v1.29.0/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the required CRDs for CloudNativePG.

steps.title: Install CloudNativePG Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the CloudNativePG operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --create-namespace --version 1.29.0

```

> Installs the operator in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.

steps.title: Create a PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a PostgreSQL cluster using a sample configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

```

> Creates a PostgreSQL cluster named 'my-postgres' with 3 instances.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: postgres.cnpg.io/v1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the PostgreSQL Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the PostgreSQL cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> This command lists all PostgreSQL clusters in the 'cloudnative-pg' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall CloudNativePG Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the CloudNativePG operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all PostgreSQL clusters managed by the operator, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg

kubectl delete namespace cloudnative-pg

```

> Uninstalls the operator from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade CloudNativePG Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your PostgreSQL data. This step will upgrade the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

helm upgrade cloudnative-pg cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg --namespace cloudnative-pg --version 1.29.0

kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg

```

> Backup current cluster configurations.
> Upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the clusters are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg -o yaml > backup-clusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: already exists'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when trying to create a cluster that already exists.

**Fix:**
Check existing clusters with 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg' and delete the existing one if necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'PostgreSQL cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the PostgreSQL instances are still initializing or have failed to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cloudnative-pg' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to PostgreSQL'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the service is not exposed correctly or the pods are not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is created and pods are running with 'kubectl get pods -n cloudnative-pg'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the PostgreSQL pods.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your cluster configuration and adjust them accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for PostgreSQL 15, improved backup and restore capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: When everything is working, you should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters -n cloudnative-pg' showing your PostgreSQL cluster with a status of 'Ready'.

kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-postgres
  namespace: cloudnative-pg
spec:
  instances: 3
  postgresql:
    version: '15'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
